channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The TikTok ban is being weighed by the Supreme Court. Here’s what to know
item.description: The Supreme Court of the United States is hearing arguments today to decide the fate of TikTok. Lawmakers believe the app, which is owned by the Chinese company ByteDance, poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: 5 GenAI principles for K-12 education
item.description: Imagine this: A new technology has arrived, drawing enormous public discussion. Among the questions is how it might be used in schools. Advocates call for its widespread adoption, saying it will revolutionize schooling and motivate students. Others are wary, fearing it will make kids lazy.
item.dc:creator: Jack Lynch, Fast Company

item.title: Farming tech is on display CES: How John Deere and others are embracing sustainability
item.description: When Russell Maichel started growing almonds, walnuts and pistachios in the 1980s, he didn’t own a cellphone. Now, a fully autonomous tractor drives through his expansive orchard, spraying pesticides and fertilizer to protect the trees that have for decades filled him with an immense sense of pride.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AI taught me to be a (slightly) better badminton player at CES
item.description: I am not what you would call a finely tuned athletic machine. I am, if anything, an outdated lawnmower engine held together by duct tape and rust. So when I was offered the opportunity to let AI help make me a better athlete at CES, I jumped at the opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: The L.A. wildfires show how social media has become just another spin room
item.description: It’s hard to remember now, as you scroll through a thicket of porn bots, anti-trans activists, and AI slop, but there was a time when Twitter was credibly labeled our “global town square.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bien-Kahn, Fast Company

item.title: Malware Attack on Macs Highlights Security Risks | Technology News
item.description: A recent malware attack on Macs has exposed the security risks associated with third-party developers and software distribution. The attack, identified by Checkpoint, used a legitimate browser download to steal sensitive information. Here's how you can protect yourself from similar attacks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UAE company to invest $20B in U.S. AI data centers
item.description: A United Arab Emirates investment firm has pledged $20 billion to build new data centers targeting AI across a number of locations across the United States.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon is selling its ad tools to other ecommerce sites
item.description: Online retailers can now pay Amazon to use its advertising service, complete with measurement and reporting tools.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S25 Slim Leaked: Specs and Launch Details
item.description: Samsung Galaxy S25 Slim leaked specs, Snapdragon 8 Elite CPU, 12GB of RAM, Android 15 OS, and possible late 2025 launch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Linux Foundation brings together top browser makers for more "open" approach
item.description: Google, Meta, Microsoft and Opera have already joined the Supporters of Chromium-Based Browsers initiative.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Enterprises willing to spend up to $250 million on gen AI, but ROI remains elusive
item.description: A sharp rise in enterprise investments in generative AI is poised to reshape business operations, with 68% of companies planning to invest between $50 million and $250 million over the next year, according to KPMG’s latest AI Quarterly Pulse Survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cohere goes ‘North’ with agentic AI
item.description: Large language model (LLM) provider Cohere has unveiled its new agentic AI offering — North — a low-code platform that will allow enterprises to build and deploy agents across different business functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 6 years ago, Elon Musk offered help during wildfires. This time he blamed DEI
item.description: When a devastating wildfire hit California in November 2018, a powerful CEO went on Twitter to ask how his company could help. That CEO’s name was Elon Musk. What a difference six years can make.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dell’s new PC names are boring—and a smart move
item.description: There are surely many reasons that the parents of tech icon Michael Dell did not name him Inspiron or XPS. For one, Dell’s parents aren’t Elon Musk. More generally, there is appeal to a commonplace name. Yes, you sacrifice originality. You risk being called a copycat by your cousin.
item.dc:creator: Katy Steinmetz, Fast Company

item.title: More than 4% PC shipment growth predicted for 2025, but not for what you expect, says IDC
item.description: PC sales certainly weren’t going gangbusters in 2024: They only grew a paltry 1% over 2023. According to new figures from IDC, vendors shipped 262.7 million PCs in 2024. But things did pick up a bit in Q4 2024: Shipments grew 1.8% over the prior year, reaching 68.9 million.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 new tools to upgrade your home office
item.description: The home office is under fire as 2025 gets underway. CEOs, politicians, and one particular billionaire have been increasingly vocal in their opposition to employees working from home.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation and nearby Wisk Aero, with ties to aerospace giant Boeing Co. and Google co-founder Larry Page, are at the forefront in the race to bring air taxis to market in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: US warns of risk from most of world’s online pharmacies
item.description: The Office of the U.S. Trade Representative warned there’s a risk of ineffective or dangerous drugs from online pharmacies that are being illegally run. In a report released Wednesday, the office singled out 19 countries that raised concerns of possible movement of counterfeit or pirated drugs.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok's fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: In a collision between free speech and national security, the Supreme Court will hear arguments over the fate of TikTok, a digital age cultural phenomenon that roughly half the U.S. population uses for entertainment and information.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta makes major move back to free speech and ends 3rd-party fact-checking program
item.description: Meta recently announced it will cease working with third-party fact-checking organizations to flag misleading posts on Instagram, Facebook and Threads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GlassFish update fixes restart hangs, random 403 responses
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has released GlassFish 7.0.21, an update of the enterprise Java application server that focuses on fixing a few tenacious bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan Links 200 Cyberattacks to Chinese Hacking Group MirrorFace
item.description: Japanese authorities reveal details of cyberattacks targeting national security and high-tech data, pointing fingers at China's MirrorFace hacking group.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 6 fast ways to free up space on your Windows PC
item.description: Want to free up space on your computer? With the right tools, you can quickly eliminate gigabytes of unnecessary files and get back to work — or whatever else you use your computer for. I’ve long argued you don’t need a “PC cleaner” app. You mostly just need the tools built right into Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Gleam 1.7 brings faster record updates
item.description: Gleam 1.7 has arrived with faster record updates and a dynamic coder for a custom type. Overall, the latest version of Gleam touts performance and package publishing improvements. This latest update to Gleam was announced January 5 and is accessible from GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Agentic AI: The top challenges and how to overcome them
item.description: As generative AI continues to surge in popularity, we are already seeing it evolve into the next generation of machine-learning-driven technology: agentic AI. With agentic AI, we are not just prompting models and receiving an answer in a simple one-step process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 ways data teams must lead in AI-driven organizations
item.description: Generative AI has inspired a surge of interest in using data to improve the accuracy of business decisions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg denies claims of building doomsday bunker in Hawaii
item.description: "I think it got blown out of proportion as if the whole ranch was some kind of doomsday bunker," Zuckerberg said.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: What to expect from NaaS in 2025
item.description: Enterprises have been slow to adopt NaaS for a number of reasons, including lack of clarity on exactly what NaaS is, lack of a solid business case that NaaS saves money over the long term, and questions around security, visibility, control, and data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Network World
